items.rationale: Treats ZK as a foundational, accelerating field for scaling and privacy; actively contributes to setup ceremonies and curated an a16z ZK canon, framing it as critical to crypto's future.

citations.quote: I just contributed to the KZG Ceremony using lazzarin.eth to help scale Ethereum
research has accelerated radically as a result of web3

items.rationale: Sees centralized social platforms as inevitably rug-pulling builders; views Farcaster as the neutral, sufficiently-decentralized platform he always wanted, with a growing ecosystem of clients and tools.

citations.quote: Farcaster is the neutral platform we wanted all along.
The Farcaster ecosystem is growing, with already a dozen interfaces and clients, and many tools

items.rationale: Loves engineering/programming games and believes fully on-chain games will fill a similar emergent-gameplay niche; collaborating closely with the a16z games team and promoting on-chain game experiments.
items.age_note: Core thesis stated 2022-06-24; reinforced via OPCraft promotion 2022-10-19, no later restatement near 2023-03-31

citations.quote: I believe fully on-chain games will fill a similar niche in coming years.
Collaborating with the games team is one of the most surreal and enjoyable parts of my week.

items.rationale: Champions wallet-based public/private keys replacing username/password and unbundling login; backs Spruce/Sign-in with Ethereum, DIDs, and neutral interoperable messaging (XMTP) as private, programmable identity infrastructure.

citations.quote: With DIDs, we can use the public keys generated by crypto wallets in more contexts, potentially replacing username/password authentication
I'm excited to see @xmtp_ satisfy this in a neutral, highly interoperable (email-like) way. It's badly needed.

items.rationale: Views L2s (optimistic and zk rollups) as the path to scaling Ethereum without compromising security; actively uses and funds zkSync and contributes to scaling ceremonies.

citations.quote: Layer 2 technologies, such as optimistic and zk rollups, aim to lower costs and scale layer 1 without compromising security
to help scale Ethereum

items.rationale: Excited about designing protocols where tokens align stakeholders, distribute risk/reward and preserve decentralization — as opposed to launching a token as a goal. Tokens as the programmable native asset of information networks.

citations.quote: Tokens are an incredible tool for growing a protocol, aligning stakeholders, distributing risk/reward, and preserving decentralization. But a token isn't a goal.
If tokens are the native asset class of information networks, they should provide the broad range of rights and powers that serve networks best.

items.rationale: Increasingly fascinated by combining AI (machines that think) with crypto (machines that own) to produce self-funding autonomous on-chain programs — e.g. the '0-person unicorn.' A clearly rising, recent theme.
items.age_note: Emerging theme: stated 2022-12-13, intensifying through 2023-03-24/31 — weight as current and accelerating

citations.quote: When will we see the first 0-person unicorn? An autonomous program on a crypto network that can fund and grow itself.
AI + crypto = how machines can think on their own

items.rationale: Excited that NFTs are programmable and can hold complex mutable state enabling interactivity (on-chain chess, replicating art) — proof NFTs aren't just JPEGs.
items.age_note: Concentrated in March 2022 NFT thread; not restated near 2023-03-31

citations.quote: NFTs are programmable... they can contain complex, mutable state to enable interactivity with people, programs, and other NFTs.

items.rationale: Sharply critical of CeFi opacity post-FTX; dismantled Binance's proof-of-reserves as useless and easily exploited, citing missing Merkle data and the fatal negative-balances flaw. Distrusts custodial/centralized crypto institutions.

citations.quote: Binance's system is useless and easily exploited by them.
Negative balances are a known fatal flaw of the "proof of reserves" method Binance shared

items.rationale: Repeatedly argues Apple's ~30% fee and policies cripple crypto's native mobile experience and suppress innovation; frames platform power as the reason mobile crypto UX is poor.

citations.quote: Apple will cripple you, block you, or take your lunch if you try to offer a native experience.
The effects of Apple's 30% fee on innovation are radically underestimated

items.rationale: Worried that centrally-controlled LLMs degrade over time and gate access; his own GPT bot became 'nearly useless,' and he criticized restricting LLaMA weights to governments/academics. Believes open source is the only way to preserve capability.

citations.quote: It has become noticeably more equivocating and didactic... It's nearly useless now. Open source is the only way to preserve the flame.
why not share the LLaMA weights publicly? Sharing only with governments and academics... was nonsense.

items.rationale: Considers 'tokenomics experts' who deliver allocation pie charts a complete waste; warns that a made-up allocation is roughly 5% of real protocol design and that launching a token isn't a goal.

citations.quote: "Tokenomics experts" who simply "work" with you to determine "token allocations" are a complete waste of time. A made up pie chart is not real work.

items.rationale: Warns developers who build atop centralized platforms get cut off, replaced, or blocked from monetizing ('rugged'); motivates his preference for neutral, autonomous, crypto-based alternatives.

citations.quote: it isn't worth it on centralized platforms — you'll get cut off, replaced by them, blocked from monetizing. Rugged.

items.rationale: Frustrated by inaccurate, misleading media coverage and poor search results around crypto, treating them as a barrier deterring reasonable newcomers.

citations.quote: Google search results for crypto are bad enough to deter any reasonable person. It's completely unacceptable.
Imagine explaining to a normal person how outrageous this coverage has been.

items.rationale: Concerned that these tokens' real economic role as the risk capital underwriting their protocols is rhetorically downplayed in favor of a 'governance' narrative — a design/communication flaw.

citations.quote: the role of MKR and AAVE/COMP as the risk token that underwrites the protocol often seems to take a back seat rhetorically to "governance"

risk_regime.rationale: Persistently a long-horizon builder-bull: insists 'we're still so early' (likens crypto to ~1995) and that underlying tech advances even in crypto winter. His optimism is structural/technological rather than a tactical trading posture, and he is explicitly risk-off on CeFi/custodial intermediaries and centralized platforms even while bullish on the underlying technology.

notes: Eddy is an engineer/protocol-design voice, not a trader — his 'market view' is about technology and design quality, not price calls. He rarely endorses individual tokens (none surfaced as genuine token excitement), so tokens_excited is empty; token mentions like ENS/DYDX/UNI/APE (2022-03-18) are descriptive airdrop-value comparisons, and UNI appears only via a16z governance delegation (2023-02-07), not as a personal bullish view. Strongest and most recent throughlines as of 2023-03-31: ZK, Farcaster/decentralized social, decentralized identity, sound protocol/token design, and a rapidly rising AI+crypto/autonomous-programs theme. Sharpest concerns cluster around centralization chokepoints — CeFi (post-FTX, Nov 2022), Apple's platform tax, and closed AI models. Recurring meta-belief: openness, user key-ownership, and neutral/autonomous infrastructure beat closed platforms; cryptography 'favors the defender' and it is 'still early.'
